format.description: Script format version. Defaults to 'atomyx/v1' when omitted.

appId.description: Bundle id (iOS) or package name (Android) of the app under test. Example: `com.example.myapp` / `com.apple.mobilesafari`. An empty string is reserved for flow fragments that run in a parent script's context.

name.description: Human-readable script name. Shown in run reports and the Studio run list.

description.description: Longer description of what this script verifies. Surfaced in reports and the Studio detail view.

precondition.description: Human-readable preconditions that must hold before running (e.g. 'user is logged out', 'device is in airplane mode'). Informational only — not enforced by the runner.

tags.description: Tags for filtering and organizing runs. Examples: 'smoke', 'regression', 'auth', 'ios-only'.

env.description: Variables available via `${varName}` in every step value. External env (e.g. CLI `--env key=value`) is merged on top of this — external wins on key collision.

proxy.description: Network-capture proxy policy. 'required' → runner validates proxy before executing any step. 'optional' (default) → capture commands fail individually if no proxy is configured.

requires.description: Flow files to run before this script (paths relative to CWD). If any required flow fails, this script is skipped.

stepDelay.description: Delay in ms inserted between each step. Default: 500 ms — gives UI time to settle after actions (animations, keyboard, network). Set to 0 for maximum speed (unit tests with MockDriver).

anyOf.description: Launch (or relaunch) the app declared at the top of the script via `appId`. First step of most entry scripts. Example: `- launchApp`.

text.description: Match element by its visible text (the string the user sees). Use for buttons, labels, menu items. YAML shorthand: a bare string like `tap: "Login"` expands to `{ text: "Login", label: "Login" }` for Flutter parity.

id.description: Match element by its stable resource id (Android `resource-id`) or accessibility id (iOS). Preferred when the app exposes them — survives i18n and copy changes. Example: `id: com.app:id/login_btn`.

label.description: Match element by its accessibility label. Flutter exposes visible text as `label`; native Android/iOS use `text`. Setting both lets priority broadening try each platform's convention without the author knowing which.

hint.description: Match element by its input hint / placeholder. Use for text fields whose only identifier is the placeholder text (e.g. `hint: "Search products"`).

role.description: Constrain the match to a semantic role. Common values: 'button', 'text-field', 'image', 'link', 'checkbox', 'switch', 'container'. Narrow broad text matches — e.g. `{ text: 'Home', role: 'button' }` avoids matching a 'Home' menu item.

nth.description: When multiple elements match, pick the nth one (0-indexed). Use sparingly — `nth` is brittle under layout changes. Prefer adding `role` or a more specific `text`.

selector.description: Element selector. Every field is optional, but at least one must be set for the selector to be useful. In YAML, a bare string shorthand — e.g. `tap: "Login"` — is equivalent to `tap: { text: "Login", label: "Login" }`.

text.description: Text to type. `${var}` placeholders are resolved against the script env before execution.

Match element by its visible text (the string the user sees). Use for buttons, labels, menu items. YAML shorthand: a bare string like `tap: "Login"` expands to `{ text: "Login", label: "Login" }` for Flutter parity.

id.description: Match element by its stable resource id (Android `resource-id`) or accessibility id (iOS). Preferred when the app exposes them — survives i18n and copy changes. Example: `id: com.app:id/login_btn`.

label.description: Match element by its accessibility label. Flutter exposes visible text as `label`; native Android/iOS use `text`. Setting both lets priority broadening try each platform's convention without the author knowing which.

hint.description: Match element by its input hint / placeholder. Use for text fields whose only identifier is the placeholder text (e.g. `hint: "Search products"`).

role.description: Constrain the match to a semantic role. Common values: 'button', 'text-field', 'image', 'link', 'checkbox', 'switch', 'container'. Narrow broad text matches — e.g. `{ text: 'Home', role: 'button' }` avoids matching a 'Home' menu item.

nth.description: When multiple elements match, pick the nth one (0-indexed). Use sparingly — `nth` is brittle under layout changes. Prefer adding `role` or a more specific `text`.

into.description: Optional selector for the target field. When omitted, `type` targets the currently focused field (after the previous step).

anyOf.description:
Type text into a field.

```yaml
- type: "hello"                       # focused field
- type: { into: "Email", text: ${email} }
```

text.description: Match element by its visible text (the string the user sees). Use for buttons, labels, menu items. YAML shorthand: a bare string like `tap: "Login"` expands to `{ text: "Login", label: "Login" }` for Flutter parity.

id.description: Match element by its stable resource id (Android `resource-id`) or accessibility id (iOS). Preferred when the app exposes them — survives i18n and copy changes. Example: `id: com.app:id/login_btn`.

label.description: Match element by its accessibility label. Flutter exposes visible text as `label`; native Android/iOS use `text`. Setting both lets priority broadening try each platform's convention without the author knowing which.

hint.description: Match element by its input hint / placeholder. Use for text fields whose only identifier is the placeholder text (e.g. `hint: "Search products"`).

role.description: Constrain the match to a semantic role. Common values: 'button', 'text-field', 'image', 'link', 'checkbox', 'switch', 'container'. Narrow broad text matches — e.g. `{ text: 'Home', role: 'button' }` avoids matching a 'Home' menu item.

nth.description: When multiple elements match, pick the nth one (0-indexed). Use sparingly — `nth` is brittle under layout changes. Prefer adding `role` or a more specific `text`.

selector.description: Element selector. Every field is optional, but at least one must be set for the selector to be useful. In YAML, a bare string shorthand — e.g. `tap: "Login"` — is equivalent to `tap: { text: "Login", label: "Login" }`.

timeoutMs.description: How long to wait for the element to appear, in ms. Default: the driver's built-in wait (usually 5000 ms).

anyOf.description:
Wait for an element to become visible. Fails the script if the element does not appear within `timeoutMs`.

```yaml
- waitFor: "Welcome"
- waitFor: { text: "Done", timeout: 10000 }
```

text.description: Match element by its visible text (the string the user sees). Use for buttons, labels, menu items. YAML shorthand: a bare string like `tap: "Login"` expands to `{ text: "Login", label: "Login" }` for Flutter parity.

id.description: Match element by its stable resource id (Android `resource-id`) or accessibility id (iOS). Preferred when the app exposes them — survives i18n and copy changes. Example: `id: com.app:id/login_btn`.

label.description: Match element by its accessibility label. Flutter exposes visible text as `label`; native Android/iOS use `text`. Setting both lets priority broadening try each platform's convention without the author knowing which.

hint.description: Match element by its input hint / placeholder. Use for text fields whose only identifier is the placeholder text (e.g. `hint: "Search products"`).

role.description: Constrain the match to a semantic role. Common values: 'button', 'text-field', 'image', 'link', 'checkbox', 'switch', 'container'. Narrow broad text matches — e.g. `{ text: 'Home', role: 'button' }` avoids matching a 'Home' menu item.

nth.description: When multiple elements match, pick the nth one (0-indexed). Use sparingly — `nth` is brittle under layout changes. Prefer adding `role` or a more specific `text`.

selector.description: Element selector. Every field is optional, but at least one must be set for the selector to be useful. In YAML, a bare string shorthand — e.g. `tap: "Login"` — is equivalent to `tap: { text: "Login", label: "Login" }`.

timeoutMs.description: Polling timeout in ms. 0 or omitted = single instant check.

anyOf.description: Assert an element is currently visible. Unlike `waitFor`, this is an assertion — the step fails (not the step's precondition) if the element is absent.

text.description: Match element by its visible text (the string the user sees). Use for buttons, labels, menu items. YAML shorthand: a bare string like `tap: "Login"` expands to `{ text: "Login", label: "Login" }` for Flutter parity.

id.description: Match element by its stable resource id (Android `resource-id`) or accessibility id (iOS). Preferred when the app exposes them — survives i18n and copy changes. Example: `id: com.app:id/login_btn`.

label.description: Match element by its accessibility label. Flutter exposes visible text as `label`; native Android/iOS use `text`. Setting both lets priority broadening try each platform's convention without the author knowing which.

hint.description: Match element by its input hint / placeholder. Use for text fields whose only identifier is the placeholder text (e.g. `hint: "Search products"`).

role.description: Constrain the match to a semantic role. Common values: 'button', 'text-field', 'image', 'link', 'checkbox', 'switch', 'container'. Narrow broad text matches — e.g. `{ text: 'Home', role: 'button' }` avoids matching a 'Home' menu item.

nth.description: When multiple elements match, pick the nth one (0-indexed). Use sparingly — `nth` is brittle under layout changes. Prefer adding `role` or a more specific `text`.

selector.description: Element selector. Every field is optional, but at least one must be set for the selector to be useful. In YAML, a bare string shorthand — e.g. `tap: "Login"` — is equivalent to `tap: { text: "Login", label: "Login" }`.

timeoutMs.description: How long to wait for the element to disappear, in ms. 0 or omitted = single instant check.

anyOf.description: Assert an element is NOT visible. Useful after dismissing a dialog or navigating away.

label.description: Filename-safe label attached to the screenshot in the run artifacts. Omit to auto-name by step index.

anyOf.description:
Capture a screenshot and attach it to the run artifacts.

```yaml
- screenshot               # auto-named
- screenshot: home_screen  # labeled
```

direction.description: Swipe direction. 'up' scrolls content up (content moves up, the view reveals what was below). Mnemonic: direction of finger travel.

anyOf.description: Swipe the screen in a cardinal direction. Targets the centre of the visible viewport. For swipes on a specific element or with custom distance, use `pointer` instead.

key.description: Key identifier. Common values: 'enter', 'back', 'home', 'menu', 'search', 'volumeUp', 'volumeDown', 'power'. Platform-specific keys are accepted — the driver maps them.

anyOf.description: Press a hardware or system key. Example: `- pressKey: enter`.
Navigate back one step. Equivalent to Android's back button / iOS's swipe-back / view-stack pop. Example: `- back`.

ms.description: Sleep duration in milliseconds. Avoid values above ~3000 — long unconditional sleeps mask timing bugs. Prefer `waitFor`.

anyOf.description: Unconditional wait. Use only when no observable signal exists (rare). Prefer `waitFor` for element-bound waits.

pattern.description: Method + path pattern to match against the captured traffic. Format: `METHOD /path`. Supports `*` wildcard. Examples: `POST /api/transfer`, `GET /users/*`.

as.description: Variable name to bind the captured request to. Referenceable later via `${name}` and by `from:` in `assertApi`/`extract`/`branch`.

anyOf.description:
Capture the next request that matches `pattern` via the network proxy. Read-only (does not modify the request). Requires proxy.

```yaml
- capture: { pattern: "POST /api/login", as: loginResp }
- capture: "POST /api/transfer as: transfer"   # shorthand
```

from.description: Variable name of a previously captured request.

body.description:
Body assertions keyed by dot-path. Each key is a JSONPath- style selector against the response body; each value is the expected match.

```yaml
body:
  "$.status": completed
  "$.user.id": ${userId}
```

anyOf.description: Assert that a captured request's response matches the given status and/or body shape. Fails the step on mismatch.

from.description: Variable name of a previously captured request.

values.description: Map of target variable name → dot-path to extract from the captured response. Extracted values are usable as `${name}` in subsequent steps.

anyOf.description:
Extract fields from a captured API response into runtime variables.

```yaml
- extract:
    from: login
    values:
      token: $.body.token
      userId: $.body.user.id
```

text.description: Match element by its visible text (the string the user sees). Use for buttons, labels, menu items. YAML shorthand: a bare string like `tap: "Login"` expands to `{ text: "Login", label: "Login" }` for Flutter parity.

id.description: Match element by its stable resource id (Android `resource-id`) or accessibility id (iOS). Preferred when the app exposes them — survives i18n and copy changes. Example: `id: com.app:id/login_btn`.

label.description: Match element by its accessibility label. Flutter exposes visible text as `label`; native Android/iOS use `text`. Setting both lets priority broadening try each platform's convention without the author knowing which.

hint.description: Match element by its input hint / placeholder. Use for text fields whose only identifier is the placeholder text (e.g. `hint: "Search products"`).

role.description: Constrain the match to a semantic role. Common values: 'button', 'text-field', 'image', 'link', 'checkbox', 'switch', 'container'. Narrow broad text matches — e.g. `{ text: 'Home', role: 'button' }` avoids matching a 'Home' menu item.

nth.description: When multiple elements match, pick the nth one (0-indexed). Use sparingly — `nth` is brittle under layout changes. Prefer adding `role` or a more specific `text`.

anyOf.description: Element selector. Every field is optional, but at least one must be set for the selector to be useful. In YAML, a bare string shorthand — e.g. `tap: "Login"` — is equivalent to `tap: { text: "Login", label: "Login" }`.

visible.description: Branch matches when this element IS visible. Shorthand: a bare string becomes `{ text, label }`.

text.description: Match element by its visible text (the string the user sees). Use for buttons, labels, menu items. YAML shorthand: a bare string like `tap: "Login"` expands to `{ text: "Login", label: "Login" }` for Flutter parity.

id.description: Match element by its stable resource id (Android `resource-id`) or accessibility id (iOS). Preferred when the app exposes them — survives i18n and copy changes. Example: `id: com.app:id/login_btn`.

label.description: Match element by its accessibility label. Flutter exposes visible text as `label`; native Android/iOS use `text`. Setting both lets priority broadening try each platform's convention without the author knowing which.

hint.description: Match element by its input hint / placeholder. Use for text fields whose only identifier is the placeholder text (e.g. `hint: "Search products"`).

role.description: Constrain the match to a semantic role. Common values: 'button', 'text-field', 'image', 'link', 'checkbox', 'switch', 'container'. Narrow broad text matches — e.g. `{ text: 'Home', role: 'button' }` avoids matching a 'Home' menu item.

nth.description: When multiple elements match, pick the nth one (0-indexed). Use sparingly — `nth` is brittle under layout changes. Prefer adding `role` or a more specific `text`.

anyOf.description: Element selector. Every field is optional, but at least one must be set for the selector to be useful. In YAML, a bare string shorthand — e.g. `tap: "Login"` — is equivalent to `tap: { text: "Login", label: "Login" }`.

notVisible.description: Branch matches when this element is NOT visible.

when.description: Condition a `handle` branch matches on. Exactly one of `visible` / `notVisible` should be set.

do.description: Steps to execute when the `when` condition holds. Either an inline list of steps, or a string path to a flow fragment file (relative to the current script).

items.description: One branch of a `handle` step: a condition plus the steps to run when that condition matches.

branches.description: Ordered list of branches. First matching `when` wins — subsequent branches are skipped for this invocation.

otherwise.description: Action when no branch matches within `timeout`. 'fail' (default) fails the step; 'skip' continues the script.

timeout.description: Max time (ms) the runner waits for any branch's `when` condition to hold. When the timeout expires without a match, `otherwise` fires. Default is chosen to cover typical screen transitions so simple scripts need no explicit `sleep` before a `handle`.

anyOf.description:
UI-based branching — probe the current screen state and run the matching branch.

```yaml
- handle:
    - when: { visible: "Enter OTP" }
      do:
        - type: "123456"
        - tap: "Verify"
    - when: { visible: "Success" }
      do:
        - screenshot: success
    - otherwise: fail
```

from.description: Variable name of a previously captured request to route on.

status.description: Match when response status equals this code.

body.description: Match when every dot-path in this map equals its expected value in the response body.

match.description: Case condition for an API-based `branch`. Conditions are ANDed together (status AND body must both match).

do.description: Steps to run when this case matches. Inline list or path to a flow fragment.

items.description: One case of a `branch` step: a match condition plus the steps to run when that case matches.

on.description: Ordered list of cases. First matching `match` wins.

default.description: Steps (inline) or flow-fragment path to run when no case matches. Omit to make a non-match a step failure.

anyOf.description:
API-based branching — route based on a captured API response.

```yaml
- branch:
    from: payment
    on:
      - match: { body: { $.requires_otp: true } }
        do:
          - waitFor: "Enter OTP"
      - match: { status: 400 }
        do:
          - screenshot: error
    default:
      - waitFor: "Success"
```

file.description: Path to the sub-flow YML file, relative to the current script's directory. The sub-flow executes in this script's context (shares appId, env, captures).

env.description: Extra variables to pass to the sub-flow. Merged over the parent env — sub-flow values win on key collision.

anyOf.description:
Execute another YML script as a sub-flow.

```yaml
- runFlow: flows/login.yml
- runFlow:
    file: flows/login.yml
    env:
      email: other@test.com
```

text.description: Match element by its visible text (the string the user sees). Use for buttons, labels, menu items. YAML shorthand: a bare string like `tap: "Login"` expands to `{ text: "Login", label: "Login" }` for Flutter parity.

id.description: Match element by its stable resource id (Android `resource-id`) or accessibility id (iOS). Preferred when the app exposes them — survives i18n and copy changes. Example: `id: com.app:id/login_btn`.

label.description: Match element by its accessibility label. Flutter exposes visible text as `label`; native Android/iOS use `text`. Setting both lets priority broadening try each platform's convention without the author knowing which.

hint.description: Match element by its input hint / placeholder. Use for text fields whose only identifier is the placeholder text (e.g. `hint: "Search products"`).

role.description: Constrain the match to a semantic role. Common values: 'button', 'text-field', 'image', 'link', 'checkbox', 'switch', 'container'. Narrow broad text matches — e.g. `{ text: 'Home', role: 'button' }` avoids matching a 'Home' menu item.

nth.description: When multiple elements match, pick the nth one (0-indexed). Use sparingly — `nth` is brittle under layout changes. Prefer adding `role` or a more specific `text`.

selector.description: Element selector. Every field is optional, but at least one must be set for the selector to be useful. In YAML, a bare string shorthand — e.g. `tap: "Login"` — is equivalent to `tap: { text: "Login", label: "Login" }`.

anyOf.description: Resolve to the centre of the matched element at dispatch time.
Absolute screen coordinates in points (the same unit the `get_ui_tree` MCP tool emits as `@cx,cy`).

target.description: Target of a `down` or `move` pointer action: a selector or an absolute coordinate.

pressure.description: Pressure applied at this action, in the range [0.0, 1.0]. Drivers without the `canPressure` capability reject scripts that set this — iOS 3D Touch, Android API 26+ stroke pressure.

anyOf.description: Touch down at the resolved target. A pointer sequence must open with `down`.

text.description: Match element by its visible text (the string the user sees). Use for buttons, labels, menu items. YAML shorthand: a bare string like `tap: "Login"` expands to `{ text: "Login", label: "Login" }` for Flutter parity.

nth.description: When multiple elements match, pick the nth one (0-indexed). Use sparingly — `nth` is brittle under layout changes. Prefer adding `role` or a more specific `text`.

selector.description: Element selector. Every field is optional, but at least one must be set for the selector to be useful. In YAML, a bare string shorthand — e.g. `tap: "Login"` — is equivalent to `tap: { text: "Login", label: "Login" }`.

anyOf.description: Resolve to the centre of the matched element at dispatch time.
Absolute screen coordinates in points (the same unit the `get_ui_tree` MCP tool emits as `@cx,cy`).

target.description: Target of a `down` or `move` pointer action: a selector or an absolute coordinate.

pressure.description: Pressure applied at this action, in the range [0.0, 1.0]. Drivers without the `canPressure` capability reject scripts that set this — iOS 3D Touch, Android API 26+ stroke pressure.

anyOf.description: Drag the active pointer to a new target. Duration per move comes from `moveDurationMs` on the enclosing `pointer` step.
Hold the current pointer position for `ms` milliseconds. Used to produce long-press and dwell gestures.
Release the pointer. A pointer sequence must close with `up`.

items.description: One primitive in a pointer sequence. W3C Actions semantics.

actions.description: Single-pointer form: one sequence the active pointer walks through. Mutually exclusive with `pointers`.

id.description: Author-chosen pointer id. Any string — used only to distinguish parallel pointers in the multi-pointer form.

text.description: Match element by its visible text (the string the user sees). Use for buttons, labels, menu items. YAML shorthand: a bare string like `tap: "Login"` expands to `{ text: "Login", label: "Login" }` for Flutter parity.

id.description: Match element by its stable resource id (Android `resource-id`) or accessibility id (iOS). Preferred when the app exposes them — survives i18n and copy changes. Example: `id: com.app:id/login_btn`.

label.description: Match element by its accessibility label. Flutter exposes visible text as `label`; native Android/iOS use `text`. Setting both lets priority broadening try each platform's convention without the author knowing which.

hint.description: Match element by its input hint / placeholder. Use for text fields whose only identifier is the placeholder text (e.g. `hint: "Search products"`).

role.description: Constrain the match to a semantic role. Common values: 'button', 'text-field', 'image', 'link', 'checkbox', 'switch', 'container'. Narrow broad text matches — e.g. `{ text: 'Home', role: 'button' }` avoids matching a 'Home' menu item.

nth.description: When multiple elements match, pick the nth one (0-indexed). Use sparingly — `nth` is brittle under layout changes. Prefer adding `role` or a more specific `text`.

selector.description: Element selector. Every field is optional, but at least one must be set for the selector to be useful. In YAML, a bare string shorthand — e.g. `tap: "Login"` — is equivalent to `tap: { text: "Login", label: "Login" }`.

anyOf.description: Resolve to the centre of the matched element at dispatch time.
Absolute screen coordinates in points (the same unit the `get_ui_tree` MCP tool emits as `@cx,cy`).

target.description: Target of a `down` or `move` pointer action: a selector or an absolute coordinate.

pressure.description: Pressure applied at this action, in the range [0.0, 1.0]. Drivers without the `canPressure` capability reject scripts that set this — iOS 3D Touch, Android API 26+ stroke pressure.

anyOf.description: Touch down at the resolved target. A pointer sequence must open with `down`.

text.description: Match element by its visible text (the string the user sees). Use for buttons, labels, menu items. YAML shorthand: a bare string like `tap: "Login"` expands to `{ text: "Login", label: "Login" }` for Flutter parity.

id.description: Match element by its stable resource id (Android `resource-id`) or accessibility id (iOS). Preferred when the app exposes them — survives i18n and copy changes. Example: `id: com.app:id/login_btn`.

label.description: Match element by its accessibility label. Flutter exposes visible text as `label`; native Android/iOS use `text`. Setting both lets priority broadening try each platform's convention without the author knowing which.

hint.description: Match element by its input hint / placeholder. Use for text fields whose only identifier is the placeholder text (e.g. `hint: "Search products"`).

role.description: Constrain the match to a semantic role. Common values: 'button', 'text-field', 'image', 'link', 'checkbox', 'switch', 'container'. Narrow broad text matches — e.g. `{ text: 'Home', role: 'button' }` avoids matching a 'Home' menu item.

nth.description: When multiple elements match, pick the nth one (0-indexed). Use sparingly — `nth` is brittle under layout changes. Prefer adding `role` or a more specific `text`.

selector.description: Element selector. Every field is optional, but at least one must be set for the selector to be useful. In YAML, a bare string shorthand — e.g. `tap: "Login"` — is equivalent to `tap: { text: "Login", label: "Login" }`.

anyOf.description: Resolve to the centre of the matched element at dispatch time.
Absolute screen coordinates in points (the same unit the `get_ui_tree` MCP tool emits as `@cx,cy`).

target.description: Target of a `down` or `move` pointer action: a selector or an absolute coordinate.

pressure.description: Pressure applied at this action, in the range [0.0, 1.0]. Drivers without the `canPressure` capability reject scripts that set this — iOS 3D Touch, Android API 26+ stroke pressure.

anyOf.description: Drag the active pointer to a new target. Duration per move comes from `moveDurationMs` on the enclosing `pointer` step.
Hold the current pointer position for `ms` milliseconds. Used to produce long-press and dwell gestures.
Release the pointer. A pointer sequence must close with `up`.

items.description: One primitive in a pointer sequence. W3C Actions semantics.

actions.description: This pointer's time-ordered action list. Must open with `down` and close with `up`.

items.description: One pointer's action list, used in the multi-pointer form (`pointer.pointers`). Pointers align on a shared wall clock anchored at the earliest `down`.

pointers.description: Multi-pointer form: parallel sequences keyed by author id. Requires the driver's `canMultiPointer` capability. Mutually exclusive with `actions`.

moveDurationMs.description: Duration (ms) of each `move` action in this gesture. Defaults to 200 ms. Shorter = flick; longer = slow drag.

anyOf.description:
General-purpose pointer gesture. Expresses every W3C Actions sequence: tap, long-press, drag, flick, pinch, rotate, multi-finger. Use when `tap`/`swipe` don't fit.

```yaml
- pointer:
    actions:
      - down: "Item A"
      - wait: 800
      - move: { x: 300, y: 600 }
      - up
    moveDurationMs: 300
```

steps.description: Ordered list of steps to execute. Each step runs after the previous one completes; `stepDelay` applies between them.

_stepLines.description: Parser metadata: 1-indexed source line for each entry in `steps`, used by run telemetry to point a failure or a live progress row at its exact position in the YAML. Underscore prefix = not part of the user-authored surface; callers do not set this — `parseScript` populates it.

ScriptDefinition.description:
Top-level Atomyx YAML script. Two physical forms share this shape:

**Entry script** — full config + steps, separated by `---`:

```yaml
appId: com.example.app
name: Login flow
env:
  email: user@test.com
---
- launchApp
- tap: "Sign in"
```

**Flow fragment** — steps only, no config. Used by `requires`, `runFlow`, and `do: file.yml`. Executes in the parent's context (appId, env, captures).
